body.title: [triage] savestate skill's `python run <verb>` fails from any cwd but the skill dir — make the doc cwd-independent
body.intention: I intend that the savestate skill's flush commands (midstream/close/close-header) run from ANY cwd — so a post-sprint or post-compact flush from the repo root doesn't silently fail with 'can't open file run'.
body.description:
**Found during the 2026-07-02 session.** skills/savestate/SKILL.md instructs `python run midstream "..."` (also `python run close`, `python run close-header`). The `run` script lives at skills/savestate/run (symlinked to ~/.claude/skills/savestate/run), so `python run` only resolves when cwd IS the skill dir. Invoked from the repo root (the normal cwd during a sprint / after /context-load), it fails: `python: can't open file '<repo>/run': [Errno 2] No such file or directory`. The flush then silently doesn't happen (the durable ticket/commit work is unaffected, but the slate ledger isn't updated). Workaround used this session: `python ~/.claude/skills/savestate/run <verb> ...`.

**Fix (doc/instruction, pick one):**
- Update skills/savestate/SKILL.md to invoke the script cwd-independently, e.g. `python "$CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR/run" <verb>` or an absolute-resolved path (the skill is always given its base dir), OR
- Add a tiny cwd-independent entrypoint the doc calls, OR
- Note explicitly that the commands must run from the skill dir and have the skill cd there first.

**Same class likely affects other skills** that use the bare `python run <verb>` idiom (grep skills/*/SKILL.md for `python run `) — fix the pattern, not just savestate, if cheap.

**Completion criteria:** following skills/savestate/SKILL.md verbatim from the repo root (cwd = repo root) successfully writes the midstream/close line to today's slate — no 'can't open file run' error.

**Test plan:** doc/skill-instruction fix — validate by running the documented command from the repo root and confirming devlab/runtime/memory/slates/<today>.slate.txt gains the line. No automated assertion form (doc correctness) -> honest close is shipped-unproven naming 'no harness for skill-doc-from-arbitrary-cwd' unless a smoke test is added.

**Affected files:** skills/savestate/SKILL.md (the `python run` invocations); possibly other skills/*/SKILL.md sharing the idiom.

**Observable via:** the slate file gaining the flushed line when the skill is run from repo root (today it silently doesn't).

**audit-emphasis:** doc-only.

**Scope boundary:** make the documented invocation cwd-independent; not a rewrite of the run script's verbs.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)

[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
